data.title: Axiata completes sale of Myanmar tower assets at revised lower price
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Axiata Group Bhd (KL:AXIATA) on Friday said it has completed the sale of its Myanmar tower operations for a revised consideration of US$90 million (RM380.4 million), relatively lower than the initially proposed US$150 million (RM713.03 million).

Axiata said the transaction was carried out via its 63%-owned subsidiary edotco Group Sdn Bhd, which disposed of its entire 87.5% stake in edotco Investments Singapore Pte Ltd — an investment holding company for edotco Group's investments in Myanmar — to Hong-Kong based Zillion Tower Holdings Ltd.

"The revised consideration has been negotiated on a willing buyer, willing seller basis, and is the result of a strategic review which included an outreach to alternative credible potential acquirers and towards ensuring certainty of deal closure," said Axiata in a filing with Bursa Malaysia.

Malaysia’s largest wireless carrier by revenue said the decision took into account the deteriorating political and economic environment in Myanmar, along with increasing exposure to potential sanctions risk. These challenges, it said, have rendered continued operations in the country untenable.

"The above, coupled with impending sanction risks, significantly affects edotco Myanmar’s operations and business, posing an insurmountable challenge to continue operating in Myanmar," added the group.

The US$90 million consideration has been fully settled in cash and is not subject to any post-completion adjustments, Axiata noted. The shares were transferred free of encumbrances, inclusive of all attached rights and entitlements.

Axiata first proposed the sale in April 2024 as part of a broader strategy to strengthen its balance sheet and redeploy capital to reduce debt. The decision followed a comprehensive review of the Myanmar operating environment, which has deteriorated sharply since the 2021 military coup.

The group, which is controlled by sovereign wealth fund Khazanah Nasional Bhd, cited multiple headwinds that led to the asset disposal, which include nationwide operational disruptions, macroeconomic strain, as well as rising sanctions risk.

This is Axiata’s second exit from a foreign market in over a year. In December 2023, the group withdrew from Nepal after a prolonged dispute involving capital gains tax liabilities tied to its former mobile unit, Ncell Axiata Bhd.

Axiata has now aligned its portfolio by grouping between long-term strategic holdings and medium-term assets identified for potential monetisation. The latter includes edotco Group itself, alongside Indonesian broadband provider Link Net, fintech platform Boost and data analytics firm ADA.

Meanwhile, its core digital telco investments — comprising CelcomDigi Bhd (KL:CDB) in Malaysia, XLSMART in Indonesia, Robi in Bangladesh, Dialog in Sri Lanka and Smart in Cambodia — will remain central to the group’s strategy of generating sustainable cash flow and enhancing shareholder returns.

Axiata shares closed four sen or 1.9% lower at RM2.06 on Friday, valuing the telecommunication group at RM18.92 billion. Year to date, shares in the company have dropped more than 14%.

Read also:
Axiata's edotco to sell Myanmar tower business for RM713m
Axiata extends longstop date for Myanmar tower sale by three months

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Global airline, travel stocks slide as Israel strikes Iran
data.description:
(June 13): Global airline stocks tumbled in premarket trading on Friday as Israel's widescale strikes on Iran drove a more than 9% surge in oil prices and prompted carriers to clear out the airspace over Israel, Iran, Iraq and Jordan.

Travel and leisure stocks also fell as Iran's retaliation raised fears of supply disruption at the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial global chokepoint for about a fifth of the world's total oil consumption.

Iran has in the past threatened to close the strait for traffic in retaliation to Western pressure.

"If oil is caught in the crossfire, we anticipate that President Trump will seek Opec spare barrels to try to keep a lid on prices and shield US consumers from the economic impact of the Middle East conflict," RBC analyst Helima Croft said.

Meanwhile, carriers scrambled to divert and cancel flights to keep passengers and crew safe, Flightradar24 data showed.

Rising global conflict zones have strained airline profitability as detours drive up fuel costs and extend flight times.

US-based Delta, Latvia's airBaltic, Greece's Aegean Airlines, Ryan Air and Air India are among a long list of carriers that have either cancelled or diverted flights from the region.

Shares of European airlines Lufthansa fell almost 5% on the Tradegate platform, while Air France-KLM and EasyJet were down as much as 4%.

Delta Air Lines, American and United slid between 4% and 5% in premarket trading.

Concerns of a prolonged disruption gripped the travel industry too.

Shares of cruise operators such as Norwegian Cruise Line and Carnival Corp, were down between 3% and 4%, while online travel agencies Booking Holdings and Expedia were down about 1.5%.

"All else equal, higher gas prices are a tax on consumers and a cost input for cruise and airline operators, which could hinder spending on all items, including travel," said Dan Wasiolek, analyst at Morningstar Research.

Conflict-driven uncertainties lifted crude prices, boosting shares of U.S. oil majors Exxon Mobil and Chevron more than 3% in premarket trade.

Stocks of top oilfield services companies SLB, Halliburton and Baker Hughes jumped between 4% and 5%.

Shares in European container giant Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd rose 3.5% and 1.2%, respectively. Tankers such as Frontline, Torm and Euronav jumped between 3.9% and 8%. 

Uploaded by Lam Seng Fatt

data.title: Abdul Wahid Omar to join IOI Corp's board as independent director
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): IOI Corp Bhd (KL:IOICORP) said former Bursa Malaysia Bhd (KL:BURSA) chairman Tan Sri Abdul Wahid Omar will join its board as a senior independent and non-executive director on June 16, 2025.

The announcement on Friday comes after Abdul Wahid's appointment to the board of Cypark Resources Bhd (KL:CYPARK) last week as its chairman.

Wahid stepped down as Bursa's chairman on April 30. He had a long association with the stock exchange, serving initially as an independent director from 2004 to 2011, before returning as its chairman in May 2020.

IOI Corp's shares closed one sen or 0.28% higher at RM3.61 on Friday, bringing the group's market capitalization to RM22.69 billion.

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: MACC forfeits over US$950,000 from Swiss account linked to 1MDB
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) said on Friday that it has successfully forfeited US$954,300 (RM4.03 million) which belonged to the late Datuk Azlin Alias, from an account in the now-defunct BSI Bank in Switzerland.

Azlin was the principal private secretary to former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, who is currently serving a jail sentence. Azlin died in a helicopter crash in April 2015.

According to the MACC, the account under the name Derivale Limited, for which Azlin was the beneficial owner, is linked to the misappropriation of 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) funds scandal.

The forfeiture was granted by High Court Judge Muniandy Kannyappan after the deputy public prosecutor informed the court that no objections or claims had been filed by any party during the proceedings on Friday.

The MACC said the forfeiture of the money was made under Section 56 of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLATFPUAA 2001).

"This marks MACC’s first successful forfeiture of overseas assets without involving prosecution," the agency said.
 

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: SST expansion threatens survival of iron and steel sector, says industry group
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Manufacturers are warning that the impending expansion of the sales and services tax (SST) on iron and steel products will place serious financial pressure on the sector and threaten its survival, an industry group said on Friday.

The timing of the SST rollout is “deeply misaligned” as the industry is already operating in a fragile and unbalanced environment — struggling with low capacity utilisation, weak demand and rising regional competition, the Malaysian Iron and Steel Industry Federation (Misif) said.

As the SST expansion will tax previously exempted items — including coking coal, steel components, steel scrap, industrial machinery, and services such as leasing and rentals — Misif warned that this would drive up costs throughout the supply chain.

“When a scrap dealer purchases scrap with 5% SST, that additional burden is not absorbed — it is passed on to steel mills. As the industry faces rising input costs, their margins tighten and they are either forced to raise prices or absorb losses, undermining their competitiveness,” the association said.

Furthermore, the expanded service tax on core services would significantly increase operational costs for manufacturers, especially those reliant on leased premises and construction services, Misif said.

These additional costs, it said, would ultimately cascade down the supply chain, burdening consumers and negatively affecting the construction sector — a key driver of steel demand, accounting for over 63% of total domestic consumption.

Misif also flagged inconsistent tax treatment of steel products, noting that some essential items such as steel wire mesh and stainless steel are now suddenly subject to up to a 10% sales tax. Even items like perforated cable trays have been slapped with a 5% SST without clear justification, adding to uncertainty and manufacturing costs.

The association criticised the lack of industry consultation prior to the announcement. With less than a month to prepare, manufacturers are struggling to carry out extensive system overhauls.

“Although the grace period until December 2025 offers some reprieve, the short lead time raises serious risks of unintended non-compliance and operational disruption,” it said.

Misif has urged the government to defer the SST expansion, allowing time for proper consultation with industry players and a comprehensive impact study to identify necessary exemptions, thresholds and a phased implementation strategy. It also called on the government to issue clear guidelines and transition support.

“These measures are vital to ensure the iron and steel industry can adapt effectively without jeopardising ongoing transformation, competitiveness or industry resilience,” it added.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Shahriman agrees he ignored calls for rights issue fundraising to the tune of RM40m for Sapura Resources
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Sapura Holdings (SHSB) director Datuk Shahriman Shamsuddin told the High Court here on Friday that in his capacity as director of Sapura Resources Berhad (KL:SAPRES) (SRB), he had ignored SHSB’s offer for SRB to carry out a rights issue that SHSB claimed would have injected RM40 million into the company for the shortfall faced by one of its main projects, the Permata Sapura project — a 52-storey skyscraper in KL known as Project Apex.

Shahriman, testifying in the winding-up petition of SHSB which was initiated by him, was subject to cross-examination from Rabindra S Nathan, the lawyer for his brother, SHSB CEO Tan Sri Shahril Shamsuddin.

Pressed on whether he had made any effort to implement the rights issue at board meetings, Shahriman said that he was against the rights issue, and therefore did not say anything in board meetings about getting this done.

Rabindra: Unless you can show me otherwise, do you agree that those minutes, all those meetings, do not reflect, record, or capture any initiative on your part or any statement on your part that would ensure SRB carried out the rights issue?

Shahriman: I was against the rights issue.

Rabindra: So you were against the rights issue, therefore you didn't say anything about getting it done?

Shahriman: Correct.

Shahriman had previously told the court that Shahril had attempted to force SRB, a subsidiary of SHSB, to carry out a rights issue by giving the company an ultimatum: pay back the RM40 million SHSB had given it as a cash injection for Project Apex shortfalls, with interests and associated costs, or go on with the proposed rights issue, which SHSB claimed would inject a sum of RM40 million into SRB for Project Apex.

At that time in 2022, SRB was facing a critical deadline for a payment obligation linked to Project Apex. SRB was required to fulfil a cash call obligation issued by KLCC Holdings Bhd (KLCCH), its joint venture partner in the project. The payment was due by Sept 30, 2022.

To avert a default, SHSB injected RM40 million into SRB on Sept 29, 2022, just one day before the deadline.

The RM40 million cash injection was part of a total RM100 million financial assistance package which SHSB had offered to SRB. However, even though SRB accepted the RM40 million as the money was needed quickly, it did not adhere to the conditions for the entire offer, resulting in a lapse of the offer, and the remainder RM60 million was not transferred to SRB.

As a result, SRB was required to immediately repay the RM40 million advanced on Sept 29, 2022, along with interests and associated costs.

In his winding-up petition, Shahriman stated that Shahril had offered to reconsider this position if SRB revisited the rights issue, which was aimed at giving all shareholders an equal opportunity to raise capital, avoid losses and cover repayment of the RM40 million loan. Shahril had emphasised that the rights issue would provide SRB with financial stability without the risk of default.

Following that, Shahriman accused his brother of pressuring SRB into accepting the RM100 million loan and proceeding with the rights issue — failing which SRB would have to immediately repay the earlier RM40 million advance.

Shahriman disagreed with Rabindra’s assertion that Sapura Group and Indera Permai Sdn Bhd (the joint venture company between SRB and KLCC Holdings to develop Project Apex) were entitled to insist that SRB fulfil its earlier commitment to carry out the rights issue.

Rabindra then asked Shahriman if the rights issue would have generated funds from existing shareholders to cover the RM40 million shortfall SRB needed for the project, with no risk of default, had it been carried out, to which Shahriman answered that the RM40 million would have gone into a “negative-cash flow” project, as he disagreed with Rabindra’s question.

Rabindra: And I'm putting it to you that instead of putting SRB at the risk of defaulting in the repayment of RM40 million, the rights issue in effect and in fact would have helped cover repayment of that amount. Do you agree, disagree, or don't know?

Shahriman: Disagree. In fact, it's the other way around.

Shahriman also disagreed with Rabindra that SRB’s own failure to get the rights issue exercise in motion to raise funds in itself created a situation of defaulting in payments.

Rabindra: I'm putting it to you that you were the managing director of SRB, which had expected on Sept 29, 2022, that it would undertake the rights issue. And despite SRB needing the funds generated by the rights issue, you did nothing to ensure that it was done. Do you agree, disagree, or don't know?

Shahriman: Disagree.

Rabindra: I put it to you that despite being the managing director of SRB, despite SRB having agreed when you were a member of the board on Sept 29, 2022, that it would undertake the rights issue, despite the passage of five and a half months, despite SRB needing the money, amongst others to cover the possibility of repayment of RM40 million, you did nothing to make sure that the rights issue was carried out. Do you agree, disagree, or don't know?

Shahriman: Disagree.

The trial before judge Leong Wai Hong will continue on July 24 with Shahriman still on the stand.

Shahriman filed the petition in September 2024 to wind up Sapura Holdings, believing it is needed for a fair asset distribution. Shahril, however, opposed this, claiming it is not a family company.

Both Shahriman and Shahril hold a 40.5% direct stake each in the company, while their equally-owned vehicle Brothers Capital Sdn Bhd has a 15% stake. Datuk Rameli Musa owns a 4% interest in the company.

Edited ByAniza Damis

data.title: FBM KLCI falls 0.56% to 1,518.11 on June 13, 2025
data.description: KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Below is a summary of how key Bursa Malaysia indices and the ringgit performed on Friday:

data.title: Ping Edge Technology ends LEAP Market debut with 52.2% gain
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Ping Edge Technology Bhd (KL:PING), a commercial kitchen equipment supplier, closed 52.2% or 12 sen higher at its trading debut on Bursa Malaysia’s LEAP Market on Friday.

Shares of Ping Edge opened at 24 sen, just one sen up or 4.4%, over the reference price of 23 sen. The company — the second to be listed on the LEAP Market this year — jumped to 35 sen by 9.30am in light trading, and stayed there till  market closed.

“The listing of Ping Edge on the LEAP Market marks an important milestone in our corporate journey — the culmination of our team’s dedication and hard work," said Ping Edge’s managing director Soh Yeow Seng.

The company is mainly involved in the trading of commercial foodservice and kitchen equipment through its online platforms. Kitchen Arena, its flagship platform, focuses on sale of new equipment, while Murah Kitchen specialises in trading pre-owned equipment.

The company offers approximately 430 brands of commercial foodservice and kitchen equipment, including Frezmac, Modelux, Unox, Fresh, Redor, Powerline, Snow, Fagor, Robot Coupe, and Aeglos.

At the last price, Ping Edge has a market capitalisation of just RM78 million and values the company at nearly 27 times its trailing earnings.

Ping Edge raised RM5.15 million from the listing through private placement. Of the proceeds, 19.4% will be allocated for setting up an additional showroom with storage facilities in Penang, 9.7% for expanding digital capabilities, 46% for working capital, and the remaining 24.9% for listing expenses.

The LEAP Market is an adviser-driven platform designed to offer emerging companies, including small and medium enterprises, greater fundraising access and visibility via the capital market. It is accessible only to sophisticated investors.

TA Securities is the IPO’s approved adviser, placement agent, and continuing adviser.

Read also:
Ping Edge already setting sights on ACE Market transfer after LEAP market debut

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title: Jetstar Asia's ops ceasing: Mavcom advises affected passengers to seek assistance
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): The Malaysian Aviation Commission (Mavcom) on Friday urged passengers affected by Jetstar Asia Airways Pte Ltd's (Jetstar Asia) upcoming operational cessation to contact the airline directly for assistance with their travel bookings. Mavcom also said consumers can reach out to its own channels for support.

This advisory follows Jetstar Group's announcement on June 11 that its Singapore-based low-cost subsidiary, Jetstar Asia, will cease operations effective July 31, 2025.

Passengers holding bookings with Jetstar Asia (3K) are advised to contact the airline via its dedicated Live Chat service at www.jetstar.com or refer to its global contact centers listed at https://www.jetstar.com/sg/en/help/contact-numbers for booking assistance.

Citing Jetstar, Mavcom said the only flights in Malaysia affected by this cessation are those operated by Jetstar Asia from Singapore, specifically services to Kuala Lumpur and Penang. The cessation of Jetstar Asia's operations does not impact any Jetstar Airways (JQ) flight, including international flights between Australia and Southeast Asia, or any Jetstar Japan (GK) flight.

"Jetstar has announced that it will directly notify customers if there are changes to scheduled flights before July 31, 2025. This is due to a planned progressive reduction in its flight schedule, leading to the cessation of services. Consumers are strongly advised to monitor their e-mail and booking status accordingly, as well as check the travel alerts section on the Jetstar website regularly for the latest updates," Mavcom said.

According to Mavcom, under the Malaysian Aviation Consumer Protection Code 2016 (MACPC), consumers affected by the cessation of operations are entitled to a full refund of the ticket cost (inclusive of taxes and fees) within 30 days, provided in the original mode of payment, or rerouting to their final destination on comparable transportation.

Passengers who do not receive these options or require further assistance are encouraged to contact Mavcom through its official channels: consumer hotline: +1800-18-6966 (Within Malaysia) or +603-7651 2777 (Outside Malaysia); email: consumer@mavcom.my or FlySmart complaint webform: https://flysmart.my/make-a-complaint/ or FlySmart mobile application that is available for download from the Google Play Store (Android) and Apple App Store (iOS).

Read also:
Singapore Airlines' Scoot to take over Jetstar routes

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: UUE bags RM83 mil worth of new contracts, pushing its order book to a record RM416 mil
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Johor-based underground utilities engineering solutions provider UUE Holdings Bhd (KL:UUE) has secured three new contracts worth RM83.4 million, further expanding its order book.

Announcement of the latest wins, secured from Sutera Utama Sdn Bhd, comes three days after UUE said it had bagged RM92.4 million worth of deals. With these additions, the company’s order book has reached a record high of RM416 million, providing earnings visibility for the next two to three years, it said in a bourse filing.

Sutera Utama, a general contractor, is jointly owned by Mohamad Zailan Melan and Wan Mohd Azmir Wan Mohd Affandi, based on a brief check with the Companies Commission Malaysia.

The new contracts involve the installation and commissioning of 11kV and 33kV underground cables and accessories within Tenaga Nasional Bhd’s (KL:TENAGA) distribution network in the northern and southern regions, according to UUE. The contracts span two years from the initial work commencement notice, with an option for a one-year extension.

“We are steadfast in our commitment to delivering each project efficiently and in full compliance with TNB’s quality standards, ultimately creating long-term value for our shareholders,” said UUE managing director Datuk Dr Ting Kok Hwa.

Shares of UUE closed down two sen or 2.7% at 73.5 sen on Friday, valuing the group at RM447.09 million. Year to date, the stock has fallen 15%.

Read also:
UUE secures RM92 mil worth of underground cable contracts

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Dialog bags Mutiara Cluster PSC in offshore Sabah from Petronas
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): Dialog Group Bhd (KL:DIALOG) has secured the asset production sharing contract (PSC) for the Mutiara Cluster Small Field located off the coast of Sabah from Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas).

The contract designates Dialog's wholly owned Dialog Resources Sdn Bhd as the sole operator with a 100% participating interest in the Mutiara Cluster, which comprises five fields — Nymphe, Nymphe North, Kuda Terbang, Benrinnes and Mutiara Hitam, according to Petronas in a statement on Friday.

The Mutiara Cluster is the first PSC awarded by Petronas, through Malaysia Petroleum Management (MPM), under the Malaysia Bid Round 2025 (MBR 2025), and is expected to achieve first production in 2029. The contract was signed on Friday (June 13).

More to come

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: US wine exports to Canada drop the most in over 20 years
data.description:
(June 13): US wine exports to Canada, its largest customer, fell by the most in more than 20 years in April as consumers and government agencies continue their boycott of American booze in retaliation against US tariffs.
 
Wine shipments from the US to the northern nation fell 93%, the largest year-over-year decrease in monthly data from the US Census Bureau going back to 2002. The next two biggest wine markets for US producers — the UK and China — also imported less in April.

The collapse contributed to a 41% global decline in US wine exports in the month, following a 28% drop in March.

“Wine is in a crisis. There is too much wine being produced, and demand has been falling,” said Karl Storchmann, executive director of the American Association of Wine Economists. “But the prevailing force right now is the tariffs and that enormous decline in wine exports to Canada.”

Canada was the largest buyer of US wine in 2024, data from the United Nations show, accounting for about a third of total export value. 

In March, Canadian provinces began removing US-made alcohol from government-run stores in response to US tariffs on Canadian goods — though some have recently resumed buying again. Canada still has a 25% import tax on US wine, which it implemented after the Trump administration started the tariff war. 

China had imposed reciprocal tariffs of 125% on American goods in April, but both countries have since lowered their tariffs and this week reached a framework to ease some trade frictions.  

In addition to the trade war, longer-term trends are influencing the market. Per-capita wine consumption dropped by more than 50% between the early 1970s and 2022, according to figures cited by the AAWE from the Annual Database of Global Wine Markets. 

Overall trade in wine has also been falling due to poor economic growth globally, said economist John Dunham. 

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: MACC seizes luxury items worth RM16m linked to highway funds misuse
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 13): The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has seized another RM16 million worth of luxury items linked to the misuse of sukuk funds for the construction of a highway in Klang Valley believed to be owned by a concession holder with the title of Tan Sri on Thursday (June 12).

The MACC said the seizure at the individual’s residence in Bandar Tasik Selatan, Cheras included 84 luxury bags worth about RM3 million; 11 branded watches worth about RM1 million; gold bars, coins and statues worth about RM3 million; and diamonds and jewellery worth about RM4 million.

“A luxury casino equipment set estimated to be worth RM150,000 was also seized,” the commission said in a statement, adding that a secret vault that stored weapons and ammunition was discovered but the weapons were not seized as they were registered under the security company the individual owned.

An Anti-Corruption Tactical Squad (ACTS) conducted the search of the individual’s residence as he was suspected to be keeping weapons in the vault.

“The MACC received information that there was a vault storing valuables, including weapons, linked to the case in his residence.

“The presence of the ACTS team was to ensure safety and to escort officers and seized items back to the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya,” the commission added.

Meanwhile, MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki confirmed the search and seizure and did not deny the possibility that there would be other seizures linked to the case.

He said that the process of taking the individual’s statement had to be postponed to a later date due to health factors.

The MACC had previously seized a total of RM143 million worth of valuables linked to the case.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Korea’s Lee urges chaebols to quell distrust, hears trade fears
data.description:
(June 13): South Korean President Lee Jae-myung called on the heads of the country’s largest conglomerates to help restore market trust, while hearing out their concerns over the impact of US President Donald Trump’s tariffs on global trade.

In his first meeting with the nation’s powerful business leaders since taking office, Lee faced a delicate balancing act, appealing to the economic clout of the chaebols, and reassuring them on trade negotiations, while signalling his intent to follow through on campaign pledges to curb their outsized influence in Asia’s fourth-largest economy. 

“Our economy can no longer sustain growth through unfair competition, special privileges for, or exploitation of some actors like it did in the past,” Lee told executives at the gathering, including the chiefs of Samsung Group, SK Group, Hyundai Motor Group, LG Group, and Lotte Group. “There is still some distrust, and I want you to help alleviate it.”

Lee, who defeated his conservative rival to become South Korea’s new president last week, has made economic revitalisation one of his top priorities. The country’s chaebols, sprawling family-controlled conglomerates, such as Samsung Electronics Co and Hyundai Motor Co, have long been a key engine of growth for the economy, giving them broad sway over business and society. 

The new president has pledged to rewrite the commercial code to weed out the rubber-stamping of corporate decisions by directors. The revised code aims to strengthen the duty of company boards to shareholders, to improve corporate governance and tackle the so-called Korea discount that has been a long-standing grievance among global investors.

But ahead of his trip to Canada to attend the Group of Seven (G7) summit, Lee found many executives at the meeting expressing more immediate concern about the impact of trade protectionism.

“In particular, US tariffs and the uncertainty surrounding the issue have created an unstable environment, making it extremely difficult for businesses to make any decisions or investment,” SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won said. 

Chey cited the intensifying US-China rivalry among the key risks facing businesses in South Korea, along with weak domestic demand, subdued investment sentiment, and an ageing population. SK’s semiconductor unit is the world’s leading artificial intelligence (AI) memory chipmaker and a close partner of Nvidia Corp. 

Samsung Electronics Co executive chairman Jay Y Lee went further, comparing the current environment to the Asian financial crisis of the late 1990s. 

South Korea remains a critical player in global supply chains, producing everything from smartphones and semiconductors to ships and electric vehicles. That makes its economy heavily dependent on trade to power growth, with exports equivalent in size to more than 40% of gross domestic product.

Hyundai Motor, one of the world’s biggest automakers, has pledged to invest US$21 billion (RM89.1 billion) in the US, something Lee can flag to Trump, should they meet in Canada. Even so, carmakers may face yet another hike in duties after Trump said on Thursday that he was considering raising auto tariffs even higher than the recently introduced 25% level, to support the industry in the US.

With the deadline for imposing reciprocal tariffs approaching early next month, Trump is keen to show progress in reaching deals with key economies that have large trade surpluses with the US. 

Talks between Washington and Seoul have been held back by the leadership vacuum and domestic political unrest before Lee’s election win.

Uploaded by Liza Shireen Koshy

data.title: Boustead Singapore conducts review of assets for potential sale to REIT for listing
data.description:
SINGAPORE (June 13): Boustead Singapore is exploring the sale of its stake in some of its logistics and industrial assets to a REIT for the potential listing on the Mainboard of the Singapore Exchange(SGX), according to a bourse filing on Thursday (June 12).

The company said that it was undertaking strategic reviews in respect of its stakes in its Singapore assets, which is part of its ordinary course of business to...(click on link for full story theedgesingapore.com)

data.title: Trump says US has a 'golden share' in US Steel
data.description:
WASHINGTON (June 13): US President Donald Trump said on Thursday the United States had a "golden share" in US Steel as part of a deal for Nippon Steel to buy the iconic American company.

It was not immediately clear what structural element of the deal Trump was referring to, but he said the share meant that Americans would have a 51% share in ownership of the steelmaker.

"We have a 'golden share', which I control, the president controls," he told reporters at the White House. "If that gives you total control, it's 51% ownership by Americans."

Nippon Steel and US Steel did not respond to requests for comment.

On Friday, a Nippon Steel executive told the Nikkei business daily the acquisition of US Steel required "a degree of management freedom" to go ahead, in a sign that last-minute discussions continue regarding the deal structure.

"The idea to contribute to the revival of US manufacturing (through Nippon Steel's investment) is in line with the US administration's policy," Nikkei quoted the unidentified executive as saying.

"Without a degree of management freedom, it may not be possible to reach an agreement with the US government."

At a rally in Pennsylvania on May 30, Trump lauded a deal between the companies, calling Nippon Steel a "great partner" for US Steel.

But he later told reporters the deal still lacked his final approval, leaving unresolved whether he would allow Nippon Steel to take ownership.

Nippon Steel and the Trump administration asked a US appeals court on June 5 for an eight-day extension of a pause in litigation, to give them more time to reach a deal for the Japanese firm to buy US Steel for US$14.9 billion (RM63.3 billion).

Nippon Steel shares closed down 1.48% in Tokyo on Friday, outstripping a fall of 0.89% in the benchmark Nikkei.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: New World’s US$11b refinancing gambit must get 100% support, Bloomberg reports
data.description:
(June 13): As distressed Hong Kong builder New World Development Co gathers more support from banks for its targeted HK$87.5 billion (US$11.1 billion or RM47.34 billion) loan refinancing, a key part of the terms shows that it must get 100% in commitments, or the deal will fall through.

Documentation for the potential borrowing shows that if New World doesn’t achieve a 100% approval rate by June 30, any collateral pledged would be released and bank commitments cancelled, people familiar with the matter said.

The terms may well wind up being moot, as New World has already garnered support for at least 87% of the total with more than two weeks remaining. But the fine print — little known by bondholders and other investors riveted by the company’s scramble to lock down funds — underscores the high stakes.

The fate of New World, controlled by one of Hong Kong’s richest families, hinges on the success of this refinancing exercise as it will provide a critical lifeline for the builder amid mounting debt pressures. Worries over its liquidity intensified since the builder delayed June coupon payments on four perpetual notes, leading to a further selloff of the bonds. 

In order to ensure that the securities remain in place for the refinancing, New World requires consent from over 50 banks to waive certain conditions on existing loans, the people said, who asked not to be identified discussing private matters. 

New World has proposed to pledge around 40 properties, including its headquarters building, New World Tower, as well as its crown jewel asset Victoria Dockside, as collateral for the mega deal. 

Failure to close the deal by June 30 means that banks could demand immediate repayment on some of New World’s existing loans as certain covenant waivers will expire, two of the people said, adding that some of its facilities will also mature. In a worst case scenario where an event of default is triggered, it could have a cascading impact on the builder’s bonds due to a cross-default clause, according to a Bloomberg Intelligence report. 

However, the fact that New World is making continued progress in winning banks’ support indicates that the deal is one step closer toward completion, the people said. 

New World didn’t respond to a request for comment. 

Meanwhile, New World is also in the market for a separate loan of as much as HK$15.6 billion backed by its key asset, Victoria Dockside, Bloomberg News reported. It is proposing to use part of the three-year facility to help pay back the HK$87.5 billion deal once the refinancing comes together.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Foreign buyers in US$10b scramble for UK takeover targets
data.description:
(June 13): Foreign buyers are feverishly snapping up UK companies at a time when London is struggling to maintain the size of its stock market and attract new listings.

Almost US$10 billion (RM42.46 billion) worth of deals targeting UK firms emerged on Monday alone this week, when US buyout firm Advent confirmed a £3.7 billion (US$5 billion or RM21.3 billion) takeover proposal for Spectris plc, a maker of precision and testing equipment and software, and Qualcomm Inc agreed a roughly US$2.4 billion deal for London-listed semiconductor company Alphawave IP Group plc. Both transactions carried premiums comfortably above 80%. 

Also, New York-listed IonQ Inc announced an acquisition of British quantum computing firm Oxford Ionics Ltd for about US$1.1 billion, while French beauty giant L’Oreal SA is taking over UK skincare brand Medik8 in a deal valued at a similar amount.

The transactions mean that the value of foreign takeovers of UK companies has risen 10% to more than US$140 billion over the past 12 months, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. That figure accounts for more than two thirds of all deals targeting UK companies during that period, the data show. 

Advisers say buyers continue to be drawn to the UK because it’s cheaper to buy companies in the country relative to other locations. The FTSE 100 index trades at 13.5 times this year’s projected earnings, while the S&P 500 trades at 22.9 times estimated profit.

“Strategics and private equity firms are making use of a good window to look at UK companies because valuations are relatively cheap,” said Khamran Ali, head of UK client coverage at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. “We’ll continue to see a lot of activity from an M&A perspective.”

‘Value trap’

A root cause of the trend, according to Charles Hall, head of research at Peel Hunt, is the continued outflow of investor money from the UK’s stock markets. Firms representing about US$100 billion worth of market capitalisation have left London in recent months, scared off by declining lower valuations and draining liquidity; investors moved money out of UK-focused equity funds for a ninth straight year in 2024 as they sought better returns in US tech stocks.  

“London listings remain stuck in a value trap, and global strategics know it,” said Oliver Scharping, a portfolio manager at Berenberg. “Domestic capital is too risk-averse to back these growth stories at scale.” 

This week’s flurry of deals follows some marquee takeovers of British companies in 2025, including DoorDash Inc’s £2.9 billion deal for London tech darling Deliveroo plc and Ageas’s £1.3 billion purchase of home and motor insurer Esure Group plc. 

To be sure, there have been UK-to-UK deals — including outsourcer Mitie Group plc’s bid for Marlowe plc and Unite Group plc’s non-binding offer for rival student landlord Empiric Student Property plc. But these have mostly been on the smaller side. 

“We expect further takeover interest in UK assets to continue,” said Patrick Sarch, head of UK public M&A at law firm White & Case LLP, who has advised clients including J Sainsbury plc. “In many cases the underlying businesses and revenues are international and well hedged.” 

Buyers in general have also been getting more comfortable with writing checks after a muted start to the year for M&A activity, which was largely stifled by US President Donald Trump’s trade agenda. 

“People are coming to terms with the Trump uncertainty and volatility as the new way of life,” said Goldman Sachs’ Ali, who earlier this was named on a list of the busiest investment bankers in the City of London. “People have decided they can’t just sit around and need to get on with things.”

Goldman Sachs is the top adviser on UK deals, Bloomberg-compiled data show. In the last 12 months, the bank has worked on deals including the private equity purchase of investment platform Hargreaves Lansdown plc and the hotly contested sale of the UK franchise of fried chicken chain Wingstop, a transaction on which Ali was involved. 

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Chinese firms in talks to join group to buy Li Ka-Shing’s ports, Bloomberg reports
data.description:
(June 13): China’s largest shipping company is among the firms in talks to invest in a multinational consortium seeking to buy billionaire Li Ka-shing’s global ports, according to people familiar with the matter, in an effort to ease Beijing’s concerns over the controversial deal. 

China Cosco Shipping Corp is one of several Chinese state-backed companies in discussions with the consortium led by Italian billionaire Gianluigi Aponte’s Terminal Investment Ltd on matters including how they might participate in the port deal, the people said, asking not to be identified discussing private information. The buying group also includes US firm BlackRock Inc and its Global Infrastructure Partners unit. 

The inclusion of Chinese investors in the consortium emerged as one of the options to advance the ports sale after high-stakes talks in Switzerland last month between Chinese and US officials, some of the people said. Beijing has fiercely opposed the sale — including two ports along the Panama Canal — over concerns it could affect its global shipping and trade ambitions. Meanwhile, President Donald Trump celebrated the deal as returning the strategic waterway to American influence. 

Once completed, the agreement to sell the two Panama ports and 41 others around the world is expected to net tycoon Li’s CK Hutchison Holdings Ltd more than US$19 billion (RM80.57 billion) in cash. 

Talks are ongoing and the details are not yet finalised, the people said. Cosco, CK Hutchison and the Aponte family’s MSC Mediterranean Shipping Co, which controls Terminal Investment, didn’t respond to requests for comment. BlackRock declined to comment.  

Cosco shares in Hong Kong extended gains to as much as 6% Friday morning, while CK Hutchison erased losses and rose 1.9%. 

The talks are the latest twist in one of billionaire Li’s most geopolitically challenging deals amid escalating tensions between the world’s two largest economies over global trade. The development has raised hopes that it could ease China’s concerns over the proposed transaction, which has been blasted by pro-Beijing newspapers as a betrayal of the nation and kowtowing to US pressure. The country’s market watchdog has vowed to review the sale, and Bloomberg News reported in March that authorities told state-owned firms to hold off on any new collaboration with businesses linked to Li and his family.

Despite the progress of the talks, a deal could still falter. A 145-day period for exclusive talks between CK Hutchison and the consortium ends in late July and the parties have already missed an initial goal of signing an agreement on the Panama part of the deal by early April. 

The current structure of the buyer consortium will give Terminal Investment ownership of all the ports except the two in Panama, whose control will go to BlackRock, Bloomberg reported in April. Terminal Investment parent MSC has 28 offices across Greater China. It runs a terminal in China’s eastern city Ningbo and operates dozens of shipping services between the country and the rest of the world. 

In an interview with the Financial Times earlier this week, the head of the Panama Canal Authority said the consortium’s structure means a concentrated terminal ownership which could threaten the waterway’s competitiveness and neutrality. In response, China’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs said it supports Panama in defending its independence and reiterated its opposition to economic bullying.

It’s unlikely that MSC will be allowed to take over all of CK Hutchison’s 43 ports due to concerns over market concentration from relevant competition authorities, said Eirik Hooper, a senior associate at maritime research consultancy Drewry. 

Still, any potential changes won’t affect the dominance for MSC, which will become the world’s largest terminal operator by throughput once the deal is completed, Hooper added. 

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane
